page: Microsoft Azure E64_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI, alongside 35 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 504.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 7.88 GB of RAM per core, but does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong memory subsystem capabilities. The server achieves a strong rating in large-block read-only memory bandwidth at 178.84 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 88.57 GB/sec, supported by a strong Passmark memory score of 2917.59. Multi-threaded operations perform well, as evidenced by a strong bzip3 decompression rate of 254.85 GB/sec and a strong Passmark database operations score of 22694.2. However, single-core CPU performance and multi-core CPU performance fall into the average tier, and LLM inference text generation speeds are weak to poor, with the 7B model generating only 6.55 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E64_v4 offers a high-density memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The lack of bundled local storage means users must rely on external block storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is well-suited for relational database management systems, in-memory caching layers, enterprise application hosting, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E64_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Ev4 family, powered by 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance features 504.0 GB of RAM, providing 7.88 GB of memory per core, with no local storage or GPU resources. Benchmark data highlights strong memory performance, with large-block read bandwidth reaching 178.84 GB/sec and strong database operations scoring 22694.2 on Passmark. While multi-threaded decompression is highly efficient, single-core CPU performance and LLM text generation speeds remain average to weak. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive applications, making it highly suitable for enterprise databases, in-memory caches, and large-scale data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64_v4 is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 504 GB of RAM. It delivers strong memory bandwidth and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB of RAM, and strong memory bandwidth performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated vCPUs and over five hundred gigabytes of RAM for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor
504.0 GB of system memory providing 7.88 GB of RAM per core
Strong memory bandwidth with 178.84 GB/sec read and 88.57 GB/sec write speeds
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for enterprise databases, in-memory caching, and multi-threaded data processing
